transcript.text: Living in Lafayette, V-Log, the 25th of May, 2013.
Today, I got up, I was working until 1.50 in the morning,
And then I got up and went and finished mowing the lawn with my friend.
And then I went over to Momaw House and worked a little bit there,
And then we went out to walk up and down Ebbinge Fine Park
And checked out the Boulder Fair, and I did some filming.
So I'll go back to Boulder maybe another time and do some other stuff,
And then I'll make a cool montage video of Boulder.
And then I just got back. We've been doing some work here and rented a movie.
I got eight popsicles at King's Heroes for a dollar.
Popsicles are great, man, in the summer.
So, yeah, I kind of have new plans. These are my plans for the summer.
Like, I'm not going to try and get work anymore.
Let's see, I'm $1,000 in credit card debt,
And then I owe my friend $9,500.
But I'm just going to try and hang out here all summer long
And not leave the house, like, even once all summer long
And not spend anything and go to the place where they give the free food twice a month
And then eat canned food
And just work on my timeline
And then try to market it and see if I can get a grant.
And if that doesn't work, by then, it'll be the beginning of the school year, September.
And a tutor doctor said that they'd hire me for Spanish lessons,
And then I'll put my own ad up about for Spanish lessons and history lessons and stuff, tutoring.
And that should be enough to do all winter long.
And then my friend said he might hook me up with some various stuff.
And I think that'll be enough, maybe, you know, if I get really good at saving money.
Living out of the van made me really good at not spending money.
And, well, living out of the van and working, it's just working all the time.
And I think I'll be able to pay my friend off bit by bit.
And I think he'll be all right with that.
And so we're going to live here through the end of September.
So next month is June.
So June, July, August, September.
We've got four months.
Four months we're going to do here.
So that's not too long.
So I'm excited.
I'm just, you know, I get excited.
Once I get into a plan and the plan is set in motion, then I can do well.
I don't have so much motivation when I'm like, I don't know what's going to happen and stuff.
So now I have a plan.
I am going to call my window washing buddies at the beginning of June just to remind them that I'm available.
I think I'm going to open up my own window washing because I love that.
But I have to get some ladders and I have the money for that.
And then you have to get the liability, which is $750 a year.
Because Mike, he said he'd do it with me.
So Mike's going to move with me into my apartment in Boulder.
So, yeah, dude, that's my plan.
I'm motivated for it.
I'm just going to ride the rollers all summer long and then work all day long.
Well, unless I'm doing the high concentration stuff.
And then I'll just be sitting on the couch.
But I think I've got some brain candy here for the next week at least.
So, yeah, right now on the website timeline, I'm just making all the pages consistent just so that all the different, you know, the CSS fonts and tags and stuff are all named the same.
And so it all looks the same.
And then making sure the links work, which takes a lot longer than I thought.
Like I keep thinking I already did it and then I didn't.
Once I fix that up, then I can go in and finish North Europe 2015 to 2000 and then the timeline explanation page.
And then once that, I'm going to do the marketing.
And I'm going to vlog all about that exactly what I do.
I'm going to, like, write all of the educational foundations, write all the 400 billionaires of America.
And then I'm going to – and then I want to just get people interested in it and know about it.
Put all the meta tags on it.
Make a Facebook page for it.
Email all the, like, history departments of the colleges and all the high schools of America.
Let me – just let everybody know about it.
And even maybe comment on all YouTube videos of people interested in history.
And all the forums and stuff.
And I'm interested to see what people think about it.
Don't look at it yet unless you, like, really want to look at it.
But I'm not going to, like, say it's ready to look at because it's not totally consistent.
When it's something all the pages aren't totally consistent can, like, throw people.
And it looks more complicated than it really is.
So that's what I'm going to do.
I'm just going to ride the rollers and work on my timeline.
I'm excited.
But I think I'm going to start making videos while I'm on the rollers.
Do that again because it just makes it so easy to make videos.
And I want to make a shitload of videos because that can help me make money too, you know.
And you never know which video makes money.
Like, you know, I've got a bunch of random videos that made a lot of money.
And they're, like, my living in the van vlog 11, 11, 20, 11.
Because it got, like, 40,000 hits or something because I typed in, like, cooking demonstrations so people looked at it.
And then it, like, got up to, like, 40,000 hits and it made me, like, 15 bucks, you know.
And I made, like, 15 bucks off of my, like, What American Thinks About Norway video and this kind of stuff.
So you never know, you know.
So I just make a shitload of videos.
Plus I like to talk.
But I want to, but before I make all my videos, I want to go into my YouTube video page on my website and just write a whole bunch of videos I want to make.
And read just a whole shitload of them so that I always have a video to make, you know.
I'm going to look and go, oh, that one, you know.
And then, of course, get in the habit of listening to audiobooks while I'm working on my timeline.
The cool thing about working on my timeline is most of it, like, once I get working on it again, it's all brain candy.
So I can, like, listen to audiobooks and stuff while I'm doing it.
So it'll be cool.
So anyway, I'm getting fired up about that.
I'll do that tomorrow.
All right.
Peace out.
New beginnings, new beginnings.
